mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following rate cuts are negative in more than 55% of cases over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, which forces equity valuations downward over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer confidence and capital expenditure growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of retail sales and housing starts fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a rank IC above 0.04 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 58% of observations when industrial production falls below its 12-month trend during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate combined with decelerating core inflation indicates successful disinflation without economic contraction, allowing the Federal Reserve to maintain accommodative policy and boosting equity valuations over one month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rate cuts and falling inflation yields negative 20-day forward returns in over 55% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in total nonfarm payrolls reflect weakening economic activity and reduced corporate profit growth, which systematically depresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following payroll declines are positive in more than 57% of cases across a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent signals robust aggregate demand and strong corporate earnings momentum, which triggers immediate algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production during periods of elevated inflation signals stagflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of observations when industrial production declines during high-inflation regimes.
